StorePolicies.title: Cancellation Policies
StorePolicies.subtitle: Define the rules for your patients when booking an appointment.

Section.title: Cancellation Policies
Section.description: Protect your time and reduce no-shows.

flexible.name: Flexible
flexible.text: Cancellations or reschedules are free of charge if made at least 24 hours prior to the scheduled appointment. Cancellations with less than 24 hours notice may be subject to a 50% charge of the consultation fee to cover the reserved time.

moderate.name: Moderate
moderate.text: A minimum of 48 hours notice is required to cancel or reschedule without charge. Cancellations made between 24 and 48 hours before the appointment will incur a 50% penalty. Cancellations with less than 24 hours notice or no-shows will be charged at 100%.

strict.name: Strict
strict.text: All appointments require a non-refundable deposit at the time of booking. If you need to reschedule, you must do so 72 hours in advance for your deposit to be transferred to the new date. No-shows will automatically forfeit the deposit.

Section.editor_label: Legal Text Visible to the Patient
Section.editor_placeholder: Write your cancellation, refund, and rescheduling policies here...
Section.editor_hint: This text will be shown to patients before confirming their appointment and in their confirmation emails.
Section.tip_desc: Having a clear cancellation policy visible at the time of booking reduces no-shows by up to 80%. Be sure to be firm but empathetic.

Cookies.title: Cookie Policy
Cookies.intro: QuHealthy uses cookies and similar technologies to improve navigation and the services of our platform.

sections.title: 1. What are Cookies?
sections.content: Cookies are small text files that are downloaded and stored on the user's device (computer, smartphone, tablet) when accessing certain web pages. They allow the platform to remember information about your visit, such as your preferred language, logged-in session, and other options.

sections.title: 2. Types of Cookies used by QuHealthy
sections.content: a) Strictly Necessary Cookies: Essential for the operation of the platform (e.g., login, load balancer, security). b) Performance and Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how visitors interact with the website, collecting metric and technical information (such as Google Analytics). c) Customization Cookies: Remember user interface settings, such as dark mode or region. d) Advertising Cookies: Allow showing relevant advertising based on your interests (if applicable in the future).

sections.title: 3. Managing and Deleting Cookies
sections.content: You can allow, block, or delete cookies installed on your device by configuring your browser options. Please note that if you disable strictly necessary cookies, some essential functions of the platform (such as bookings) may not operate correctly.

PublicPrivacy.s1_desc: This Privacy Notice applies to all QuHealthy platform users — patients, doctors, clinics, and website visitors. It describes what personal data we collect, how we use it, and the rights available to you. Creating an account or booking an appointment means you've had the opportunity to review this notice. For sensitive health information specifically, we ask for your separate, explicit consent at the point you provide it, as described in Section 7.
PublicPrivacy.s2_desc: We collect different categories of information to operate the platform:
PublicPrivacy.s2_l1: Identity Data: Name, email, date of birth, and ID document.
PublicPrivacy.s2_l2: Health Information: Medical history, lab results, consultation notes, and prescriptions, entered by you or your healthcare provider. Under Mexican law, this is classified as sensitive personal data (datos personales sensibles); in a U.S. HIPAA-covered context, the equivalent term is Protected Health Information. Either way, it receives our highest level of protection and requires your express consent.
PublicPrivacy.s2_l3: Technical Data: IP address, browser type, operating system, and device identifiers, collected through cookies subject to your consent — see our Cookie Policy for details.
PublicPrivacy.s3_desc: We use your health data exclusively to facilitate medical care. QuHealthy never sells medical history to third parties, pharmaceutical companies, or insurance companies, under any circumstances. Technical data is used, with your consent, for performance analytics and platform improvements.
PublicPrivacy.s4_desc: Your health information is only shared with the healthcare professionals you choose to book an appointment with, or to whom you grant direct access from your patient dashboard. We may share anonymized, aggregated data — where it's not possible to identify you — with medical research institutions, under contractual confidentiality obligations. We never share your health information with advertisers, data brokers, or social media companies.
PublicPrivacy.s5_desc: The platform is built under strict cybersecurity standards. Sensitive data, including medical records and media (X-rays, photos), is stored with AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit. We host on cloud infrastructure that offers HIPAA-eligible services. As we formalize healthcare relationships in the United States, we will pursue and clearly disclose any additional compliance certifications we obtain.
PublicPrivacy.s6_desc: Some of our service providers — for example, payment processing and cloud infrastructure — may process data outside Mexico, including in the United States. Where this happens, we put contractual safeguards in place to protect your information to the standard described in this notice. We retain personal data only as long as necessary to provide the service, meet legal record-keeping obligations, or resolve disputes; once that period ends, data is securely deleted or anonymized.
PublicPrivacy.s7_desc: Depending on your location, you have rights over your personal data under Mexico's Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties (LFPDPPP), the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), the EU's GDPR, or other applicable local law. In Mexico and most of Latin America, these are known as ARCO rights:
PublicPrivacy.s7_l1: Acceso (Access): Request a complete copy of the personal data we hold about you, in a machine-readable format.
PublicPrivacy.s7_l2: Rectificación (Rectification): Request correction of inaccurate or outdated information.
PublicPrivacy.s7_l3: Cancelación (Cancellation): Request deletion of your account and associated data — comparable to the 'right to be forgotten' — where there's no legal reason for us to keep it.
PublicPrivacy.s7_l4: Oposición (Objection): Object to certain uses of your data, and revoke access you've previously granted to doctors or clinics.
PublicPrivacy.s7_note: If you're in Mexico and believe your rights haven't been respected, you can file a complaint with the Secretaría Anticorrupción y Buen Gobierno (Dirección General de Datos Personales en el Sector Privado), Mexico's data protection authority.
PublicPrivacy.s8_desc: QuHealthy is not directed at, and we do not knowingly collect personal information directly from, individuals under 18. If a minor receives care booked through QuHealthy, this occurs under the account, supervision, and consent of a parent or legal guardian.
PublicPrivacy.contact: To exercise your privacy rights (ARCO, CCPA, or otherwise) or for questions about our security practices, contact our privacy team at privacy@quhealthy.com. Data controller: [QuHealthy legal entity name and registered address — to be completed upon RFC registration].

PublicCookies.title: Cookie Policy
PublicCookies.subtitle: Full transparency on how we use tracking technologies to protect your privacy and improve your experience — across Mexico, the United States, and Latin America.
PublicCookies.intro_title: 1. What Are Cookies and Other Tracking Technologies?
PublicCookies.intro_desc:
Cookies are small text files that websites store on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile) when you visit them. QuHealthy also uses similar technologies — such as local storage — to make sure the platform works correctly, understand how you interact with it, and, where you've given consent, improve your experience.

This Cookie Policy works alongside our Privacy Notice (Aviso de Privacidad), which explains in full how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal data — including health-related data. Where the two documents overlap, the Privacy Notice governs.
PublicCookies.legal_desc:
Because QuHealthy serves healthcare professionals and patients across Mexico, with expansion planned across Latin America and the United States, this policy is built to apply the strictest relevant standard wherever frameworks differ — in practice, that means treating health-related data with heightened protection no matter where you're located.

**Mexico.** We comply with the Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares (LFPDPPP), republished in the Diario Oficial de la Federación on March 20, 2025, and supervised by the Secretaría Anticorrupción y Buen Gobierno (Dirección General de Datos Personales en el Sector Privado), following the dissolution of INAI. Under this law, health-related information is datos personales sensibles (sensitive personal data), which requires your express, written consent before we process it — not just a passive cookie banner. You have ARCO rights (Acceso, Rectificación, Cancelación, Oposición), described in Section 6.

**United States.** Where applicable, we comply with the California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act (CCPA/CPRA) and comparable state comprehensive privacy laws, as well as state laws that specifically govern "consumer health data" (such as Washington's My Health My Data Act). We follow FTC guidance under the Health Breach Notification Rule, which restricts sharing health information with third parties — including via tracking pixels — without proper authorization.

**Latin America.** As we expand, we intend to comply with each country's applicable data protection law (e.g., Brazil's LGPD, Colombia's Ley 1581, Argentina's Ley 25.326, and others as relevant). This policy reflects general best practices shared across these frameworks; country-specific addenda will be published as we launch in each market.

*This section is a good-faith summary for transparency, not legal advice. It should be reviewed by local counsel before being relied on for compliance purposes.*
PublicCookies.types_essential_desc: Vital for the platform to function: keeping you securely logged in, routing, load balancing, and fraud prevention. These can't be disabled — under every framework above, they don't require consent because the service can't function without them.
PublicCookies.types_functional_desc: Remember choices you've made, like language or display settings, so you don't have to reset them on every visit. These are optional and only activate with your consent.
PublicCookies.types_analytics_desc: Help us understand how people navigate QuHealthy, which pages get used, and where errors occur, so we can improve the product. We use Google Analytics with IP anonymization enabled. We don't use analytics data to make inferences about anyone's health status, and we configure our analytics tooling to avoid capturing data from clinical or appointment-related screens wherever feasible.
PublicCookies.types_marketing_desc:
QuHealthy does not currently use third-party advertising or remarketing pixels — including Meta Pixel — anywhere on the platform, including on public marketing pages. We made this decision deliberately: tools like this have been the subject of major regulatory action and litigation in the health sector for transmitting health-related browsing behavior to ad networks without proper authorization, and we'd rather not take that risk with your data.

If we introduce any conversion-tracking or remarketing tool in the future, it will be:
- Limited strictly to public, non-clinical marketing pages — never search, booking, or account areas
- Activated only with your separate, explicit opt-in consent
- Covered by a written data processing agreement prohibiting the vendor from using or inferring health information
PublicCookies.duration_desc: We'll keep this table current as our tooling changes. For a full, itemized list of cookies currently active on the platform, contact us using the details in Section 11.
PublicCookies.sensitive_desc:
Some information QuHealthy processes — such as health conditions, the specialties you search for, or appointment details — is treated as sensitive personal data under Mexican law and under equivalent "consumer health data" categories in U.S. state law. For this category of data, regardless of where you're located:

- We obtain your express, affirmative consent before processing it for anything beyond delivering the service you requested.
- We do not sell this information, and we do not share it with advertisers or data brokers.
- We apply additional internal access controls, separate from general account access.

Full details live in our Privacy Notice / Aviso de Privacidad — this Cookie Policy only covers the tracking-technology slice of that broader commitment.
PublicCookies.rights_desc:
**If you're in Mexico or elsewhere in Latin America (ARCO rights):**
You can request to Access the personal data we hold about you, Rectify it if inaccurate, request Cancellation (deletion) when there's no legal reason to keep it, and Oppose certain uses. Email us using the contact details in Section 11 — we'll verify your identity and respond within the timeframe required by applicable law.

**If you're in the United States (CCPA/CPRA and similar state laws):**
You have the right to know what personal information we collect, request deletion, correct inaccurate information, and opt out of the "sale" or "sharing" of personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising. We do not currently sell or share personal information as those terms are defined under CCPA. We honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals where your browser supports them.

**Everyone:**
You can withdraw consent for any non-essential cookie at any time using the preference manager described below, without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out before your withdrawal.
PublicCookies.consent_desc:
When you first visit QuHealthy, you'll see a cookie banner letting you accept or reject each non-essential category individually — we don't bundle marketing consent together with analytics, or analytics with functional cookies. You can change your choices at any time using the Manage Cookie Preferences button at the bottom of this page.

You can also control cookies directly through your browser settings (to block, delete, or get warned about cookies), though doing so may affect platform functionality. Most browsers explain how under "Privacy" or "Security" in their help documentation.
PublicCookies.thirdparty_desc:
Some third-party services we work with may set their own cookies or process data on our behalf, including:

- Stripe — payment processing
- Google Analytics — usage analytics, IP-anonymized

These providers are bound by contract to protect your data and use it only for purposes we've authorized. Some of them may process data outside Mexico or Latin America (for example, in the United States). Where that happens, we work to put appropriate contractual safeguards in place.

We do not currently work with any advertising network or data broker, and none of our third-party providers receive health information for advertising purposes.
PublicCookies.children_desc: QuHealthy is not directed at, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from, individuals under 18. If you're a parent or guardian booking care for a minor, you do so through your own account, under your own consent.
PublicCookies.changes_desc: We'll update this policy as our practices, the law, or our footprint across Mexico, the U.S., and Latin America evolve. We'll note the date of the last update at the top of this page, and for material changes, we'll provide more prominent notice (such as a banner or email).
